diagnosis: Default --count of 10000 frames in registry is far too high; actual send rate is ~22 pkt/s (not the configured 100/s), so even the test-supplied 5000 frames took 226s against a 30s timeout. Reducing the registry default to 500 frames yields ~23s at the observed rate.

files_changed.description: Reduced default count for mac-flooding from 10000 to 500 so the attack completes in under 25s at the observed ~22 pkt/s send rate

notes: The actual packet send rate (~22 pkt/s) is much lower than the configured rate (100/s) because scapy's sendp() plus time.sleep(1/rate) incurs overhead per frame inside a Python loop. The registry default is the only thing controlling the default workload when --count is not supplied on the CLI. No source logic was changed; only the registry default value. requires_rebuild is false because registry.py is pure Python loaded at runtime — no Docker image rebuild is needed.
